name: Cameron Brown
abstract: Cameron Langdon Brown (born December 21, 1945) is an American jazz double bassist known for his association with the Don Pullen/George Adams Quartet.

concerts.label: An Evening with Jon Lucien, featuring Special Guests Stanley Turrentine and George Benson
Midnight Performance: An Evening with Jon Lucien

about_quotes.speaker: Sheila Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve gotten back together with Cameron Brown. Because originally he had rehearsed bass and voice with me... We’ll do it. We just did it last week in Europe
about_quotes.doc_title: Sheila Jordan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Middleburgh, New York, May 11, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Jane Ira Bloom
about_quotes.evidence: Continuing to play with my trio with Bobby Previte on drums, Cameron Brown will be playing bass
about_quotes.doc_title: Jane Ira Bloom interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 3, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: Got to Stockholm. Had a name from Cameron Brown, who had lived in Sweden. The first name. This was Lars Werner. Called him up.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: Don Cherry had joined George Russell’s group, and Cameron Brown on bass, and a cat from the Scandinavian countries
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
